name: Cranberry Cream Chees Mold
recipeIngredient: 1 package (8 servings or 2 packages for 4 servings) Jell-O in cranberry flavor or any red flavor
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir boiling water into gelatin in a large bowl for at least 2 minutes until completely dissolved.
2. Add cold water and cinnamon to the gelatin mixture. Set aside at room temperature.
3. Refrigerate the remaining gelatin about 1 1/2 hours or until thickened (a spoon drawn through leaves an impression on the gelatin).
4. Stir apple and cranberry sauce into the thickened gelatin.
5. Spoon the mixture into a 6-cup mold and refrigerate for about 30 minutes, or until set but not firm (the gelatin should stick to your finger when touched and form a mound).
6. In a medium bowl, stir cream cheese with a wire whisk until smooth. Gradually fold in the reserved 1 cup of thickened gelatin.
7. Pour the cream cheese mixture over the gelatin layer in the mold.
8. Refrigerate for 4 hours or until firm.
9. Unmold and garnish as desired.
Makes 12 servings.
Note: To prepare without a cream cheese layer, omit the cream cheese. Chill all of the gelatin about 1 1/2 hours or until thickened before adding apple and cranberry sauce. Pour into the mold and refrigerate.
